content.text: Everything checks out: SANs cover `es-http.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local`, and `vm.max_map_count` is already 262144. Transport will use `verification_mode: certificate` since pod FQDNs don't match the wildcard. Writing manifests.

input.content:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: es-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |
    cluster.name: es-cluster
    network.host: 0.0.0.0
    discovery.seed_hosts: es-transport
    cluster.initial_master_nodes: es-0,es-1,es-2
    xpack.security.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-transport
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  publishNotReadyAddresses: true
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
    - name: transport
      port: 9300
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-http
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
    - name: https
      port: 9200
      targetPort: 9200
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: es-pdb
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: es
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  serviceName: es-transport
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: elasticsearch
    spec:
      securityContext:
        fsGroup: 1000
      containers:
        - name: elasticsearch
          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
          ports:
            - name: https
              containerPort: 9200
            - name: transport
              containerPort: 9300
          env:
            - name: node.name
              valueFrom:
                fieldRef:
                  fieldPath: metadata.name
            - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
              value: "-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
            - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: elastic-password
                  key: password
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 250m
              memory: 1Gi
            limits:
              memory: 2Gi
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
            - name: config
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
              subPath: elasticsearch.yml
            - name: certs
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs
              readOnly: true
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command:
                - bash
                - -c
                - >-
                  curl -s -k -u elastic:$ELASTIC_PASSWORD
                  https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health
                  | grep -qE '"status":"(green|yellow)"'
            initialDelaySeconds: 20
            periodSeconds: 10
            timeoutSeconds: 5
            failureThreshold: 30
      volumes:
        - name: config
          configMap:
            name: es-config
        - name: certs
          secret:
            secretName: es-http-tls
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: data
      spec:
        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 2Gi

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 40); do
  ready=$(kubectl get statefulset es -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}' 2>/dev/null)
  echo "[$i] readyReplicas=${ready:-0}"
  if [ "$ready" = "3" ]; then echo "ALL READY"; break; fi
  sleep 15
done
echo "=== pods ==="; kubectl get pods -n elasticsearch -l app=elasticsearch -o wide

content.text: Classic deadlock: ordered StatefulSet startup — es-0 won't become Ready until a cluster forms, but the cluster needs es-1/es-2 which never start. Fix: `podManagementPolicy: Parallel`. It's immutable, so I'll recreate the StatefulSet.

        - name: config
          configMap:
            name: es-config
        - name: certs
          secret:
            secretName: es-http-tls
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: data
      spec:
        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 2Gi

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 40); do
  ready=$(kubectl get statefulset es -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}' 2>/dev/null)
  echo "[$i] readyReplicas=${ready:-0} $(date +%T)"
  if [ "$ready" = "3" ]; then echo "ALL READY"; break; fi
  sleep 15
done
kubectl get pods -n elasticsearch -l app=elasticsearch

content.text: All 3 nodes Ready. Now verify HTTPS reachability through the `es-http` Service, cluster health, and read/write.

input.command:
PW=$(kubectl get secret elastic-password -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d)
run() { kubectl exec curl-test -n elasticsearch -- sh -c "$1"; }
echo "=== cluster health (via es-http Service) ==="
run "curl -s -k -u elastic:$PW https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local:9200/_cluster/health?pretty"
echo "=== node count ==="
run "curl -s -k -u elastic:$PW https://es-http:9200/_cat/nodes?v"

content.text: 3-node green cluster. Now verify write and read.

input.command:
PW=$(kubectl get secret elastic-password -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d)
run() { kubectl exec curl-test -n elasticsearch -- sh -c "$1"; }
echo "=== WRITE ==="
run "curl -s -k -u elastic:$PW -XPOST https://es-http:9200/karma-test/_doc/1?refresh -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{\"msg\":\"hello\",\"n\":42}'"
echo; echo "=== READ ==="
run "curl -s -k -u elastic:$PW https://es-http:9200/karma-test/_doc/1?pretty"
echo "=== TLS cert served (verify hostname) ==="
run "curl -sv -k -u elastic:$PW https://es-http:9200/ 2>&1 | grep -iE 'subject:|issuer:' | head"
